takeaways: Senior housing M&A stayed hot in Q1:26 with 231 deals (+25.5% YoY) even as total disclosed value fell to $3.52B; assisted living made up 46.8% of volume — a green light for operators evaluating portfolio recycling.

The Fed held at 3.50%–3.75% in March and meets again April 29; 10-year Treasury has stabilized around 4.00%–4.25%, giving senior housing borrowers the most constructive financing backdrop in three years.

Agentic AI is moving from pilot to production — OpenAI, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Salesforce and EY all shipped enterprise agent platforms this month, signaling that operators delaying AI adoption risk falling behind on underwriting, leasing and operations.

items.headline: Senior housing M&A hits 231 deals in Q1:26, up 25.5% year-over-year
items.summary: LevinPro LTC data shows 231 publicly announced seniors housing and care acquisitions in Q1:26 — down 19.8% from Q4:25's 288 but up 25.5% from Q1:25. Assisted living made up 46.8% of deals, skilled nursing 36%. Total disclosed value fell to $3.52B, implying more small- and mid-size portfolio trades — a favorable backdrop for Lloyd Jones acquisition pipeline.

items.headline: NIC projects industry occupancy above 90% by end of 2026
items.summary: Primary-market senior housing occupancy reached 88.7% in Q3:25 and JLL put Q4:25 at 89.9%. Independent living crossed 90% for the first time since 2019 and active adult sits at 92.1%. Net absorption has outpaced new openings for 17 straight quarters, with only ~1,500 new units added in Q3:25 — the lowest on record.

items.headline: Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%; next decision April 29
items.summary: The FOMC voted 11-1 in March to hold the benchmark rate at 3.50%–3.75% and updated its dot plot to one additional cut in 2026 and another in 2027. Core PCE inflation is projected at 2.7%. The April 29 meeting could reset the cost-of-capital narrative for senior housing refinancings maturing in 2026–2027.

items.headline: CRE debt markets reopen: Colliers sees sales volume up 15–20% in 2026
items.summary: The 10-year Treasury has stabilized around 4.00%–4.25% since mid-2025, and CBRE/Colliers forecast a 15–20% jump in CRE sales activity this year as institutional and cross-border capital return. Cap rates for most property types are expected to compress 5–15 bps, with good-quality assets leading the tightening — supportive of senior housing valuations on exit.

items.headline: EY deploys agentic AI across 130,000-person audit workforce
items.summary: EY announced a global rollout of enterprise-scale agentic AI across its entire Assurance workforce — 130,000 professionals running 160,000 audits in 150+ countries. The multi-agent framework is built on Microsoft Azure Foundry and embedded directly in EY Canvas. For operators, this signals that audit, compliance and lease abstraction workflows are about to compress dramatically.

items.headline: NVIDIA launches enterprise Agent Toolkit with Adobe, Salesforce, SAP + 14 others
items.summary: At GTC 2026, NVIDIA unveiled its open Agent Toolkit with 17 launch partners including Adobe, Salesforce and SAP. The platform targets knowledge work automation — procurement, lease administration, resident services and underwriting are all in scope. Real estate owners benchmarking AI vendors should treat this as the new baseline reference architecture.

items.headline: U.S. News names 1,614 communities to 2026 Best Senior Living list
items.summary: U.S. News & World Report released its 2026 Best Senior Living ratings on April 14, naming 1,614 of the 3,000+ communities assessed. Bridge Senior Living alone earned 38 honors across 9 communities. The 80+ population is expected to grow ~25% over the next five years and double by 2035 — confirming the demographic tailwind thesis.

items.headline: Luxury senior living project The Riviera opens in Alexandria, VA
items.summary: The first residents moved into The Riviera, a new luxury senior living building in Alexandria, Virginia, with the ribbon-cutting in April. The opening joins a short list of premium lease-ups testing the top end of private-pay demand in high-income Mid-Atlantic submarkets — a useful comp for Lloyd Jones underwriting in competing markets.

items.headline: CRE loan rates run 5.07%–8.75% for conventional mortgages in April
items.summary: As of April 16, conventional commercial mortgage rates range from 5.07% to 8.75% depending on property type and borrower credit, with the broader CRE loan market running 4.87%–12.75%. Lender liquidity is described as ample with tight spreads — operators should be actively revisiting refi strategy and lender rosters.

items.headline: OpenAI ships updated Agents SDK with sandbox and harness capabilities
items.summary: OpenAI expanded its Agents SDK with new harness and sandbox features aimed at enterprise deployments, launching first in Python with TypeScript to follow. Combined with the new Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 (stable APIs, long-term support, full MCP support), the enterprise agent stack is consolidating fast around a few standards.

items.headline: Salesforce unveils 'Headless 360' to embed AI agents across enterprise
items.summary: At TrailblazerDX on April 15, Salesforce announced Headless 360, an API-first platform built for what it calls the 'agentic enterprise' — where humans and AI agents share workflows. For senior housing operators on Salesforce, the update materially expands what can be automated in CRM, lead nurture and resident onboarding.

items.headline: 71% of leaders report elevated stress; 40% considering leaving
items.summary: DDI's 2026 leadership report finds 71% of leaders under increased stress and 40% weighing a departure, as U.S. layoffs hit a 10-year high. 'Quiet cracking' — low-visibility burnout — is named a top 2026 trend. Senior housing operators running lean corporate teams should revisit retention, succession and span-of-control plans now.

items.headline: BayCom Corp announces executive leadership transition effective April 13
items.summary: BayCom Corp's Board approved a leadership transition and appointed three new executives effective April 13, 2026, while outlining a next-phase growth strategy. A timely reminder that community bank C-suite changes can shift construction and bridge lending appetite for senior housing borrowers in the West.

items.headline: Human + AI leadership named the defining management shift of 2026
items.summary: Multiple 2026 outlooks converge on 'human + AI leadership' as the year's most important capability, alongside flatter org structures and job-hugging (stalled internal mobility). The premium is on leaders who build continuous-learning strategy systems rather than static annual plans — a mindset shift for operators still running traditional 3-year strategic cycles.

items.headline: 89% of employees say wellbeing drives their performance
items.summary: Wellhub's State of Work-Life-Wellness 2026 finds 89% of employees perform better when prioritizing wellbeing, marking a shift from wellness-as-benefit to wellness-as-productivity-lever. 'Third places' — gyms, yoga studios, wellness hubs — are replacing the office as workplace gathering points, with clear design implications for active adult and IL amenity packages.

items.headline: AI-driven 'personalized wellness' named top 2026 trend
items.summary: 2026 is being called a turning point for personalized wellness thanks to AI analytics, at-home biomarker tests and next-gen wearables that track far beyond sleep and steps. For senior living operators, this opens a real path to packaging data-informed wellness programming as a premium amenity for IL and active adult residents.

items.headline: Proptech tool consolidation accelerates as operators demand ROI
items.summary: ICSC reports real estate owners are rationalizing proptech stacks — 2026 is 'less about adopting more tech and more about making the existing stack work end to end.' Tenant relationship management, lease drafting and portfolio management top the list of AI-ready workflows. Expect vendor consolidation to accelerate through H2:26.

items.headline: Senior living enters 2026 'at a critical juncture,' McKnight's reports
items.summary: McKnight's Senior Living characterizes the year as a critical juncture balancing surging demand against workforce, capex and regulatory pressure. With demand at an all-time high per NIC data, operators who can scale staffing and expand capex for modernization should capture disproportionate share of the 80+ demographic wave.

items.headline: 'Movement snacks' emerge as headline wellness trend for 2026
items.summary: Short bursts of movement, stretching and mobility scattered through the day — 'movement snacks' — are being positioned as the 2026 antidote to prolonged sitting, helping joint health and metabolism. Easy to integrate into IL/AL programming calendars and a low-cost differentiator for communities targeting active, health-conscious residents.

items.headline: Emotional fitness named the new health category of 2026
items.summary: Industry outlooks are elevating 'emotional fitness' — cognitive performance plus emotional resilience — to a standalone health category alongside physical fitness and nutrition. For senior housing operators, this reframes memory care and social programming as core wellness infrastructure rather than ancillary services.
